pull.title: Add new Mintlify documentation site (docs_new/)
pull.body:
## Motivation

Migrate and consolidate the SGLang documentation from the separate [sgl-docs](https://github.com/sgl-project/sgl-docs) and [sgl-cookbook](https://github.com/sgl-project/sgl-cookbook) repositories into the main sglang repo under docs_new/. This provides a single source of truth for all SGLang documentation, powered by Mintlify, and simplifies the maintenance workflow by eliminating cross-repo syncing.

## Modifications

- Add docs_new/ directory containing the full Mintlify-based documentation site (319 files, ~97k lines)
- Getting Started: Introduction, installation, quickstart guides
- Model docs: LLM, VLM, Diffusion, Embedding, Reward, Rerank, Classification with model-specific guides (DeepSeek, Qwen, GLM, Ernie, Chroma, etc.)
- Hardware platforms: NVIDIA GPUs, AMD GPUs, Ascend NPU, TPU, NVIDIA Jetson Orin, XPU, CPU
- Developer guide: Contributing guide, extending SGLang
- Cookbook: Migrated examples and benchmark cards from sgl-cookbook
- CI: GitHub Actions workflow for syncing LMSYS SGLang blog cards
- All changes are scoped to docs_new/ — no modifications to existing code or docs

## Accuracy Tests

N/A — documentation only, no changes to model or kernel code.

## Speed Tests and Profiling

N/A — documentation only, no changes to inference code.

## Checklist

- [x] Format your code according to the [Format code with pre-commit](https://docs.sglang.io/developer_guide/contribution_guide.html#format-code-with-pre-commit).
- [ ] Add unit tests according to the [Run and add unit tests](https://docs.sglang.io/developer_guide/contribution_guide.html#run-and-add-unit-tests).
- [x] Update documentation according to [Write documentations](https://docs.sglang.io/developer_guide/contribution_guide.html#write-documentations).
- [ ] Provide accuracy and speed benchmark results according to [Test the accuracy](https://docs.sglang.io/developer_guide/contribution_guide.html#test-the-accuracy) and [Benchmark the speed](https://docs.sglang.io/developer_guide/contribution_guide.html#benchmark-the-speed).
- [x] Follow the SGLang code style [guidance](https://docs.sglang.io/developer_guide/contribution_guide.html#code-style-guidance).

## Review and Merge Process

1. Ping Merge Oncalls to start the process. See the [PR Merge Process](https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang/blob/main/.github/MAINTAINER.md#pull-request-merge-process).
2. Get approvals from [CODEOWNERS](https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang/blob/main/.github/CODEOWNERS) and other reviewers.
3. Trigger CI tests with [comments](https://docs.sglang.io/developer_guide/contribution_guide.html#how-to-trigger-ci-tests) or contact authorized users to do so.
   - Common commands include `/tag-and-rerun-ci`, `/tag-run-ci-label`, `/rerun-failed-ci`
4. After green CI and required approvals, ask Merge Oncalls or people with Write permission to merge the PR.
